verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation. The protein body faces the cytosol; no part of SRC is exposed to the extracellular space. Walking the contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet display in cancer, cell death, infection, or activation; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no narrow germline/developmental ecto-pool; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — endosomal/perinuclear pools exist but all cytoplasmic-facing, no surface minority pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering with an extracellular face. Clinical SRC inhibitors (dasatinib, bosutinib) are intracellular ATP-pocket small molecules.
